channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania to Expand Air Tanzania Fleet to 24 Aircraft by 2030
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced a plan to expand the national airline, Air Tanzania Company Limited (ATCL), by adding eight new aircraft to its current fleet, targeting a total of 24 planes b...

item.title: Australia’s Media Needs Broader Global Perspectives on the Middle East
item.description: Christopher Warren argues that Australia’s mainstream media has become too dependent on English-language news produced in the US and the UK, especially in times of global crisis like the current war o...

item.title: Pakistan Meteorological Department forecasts widespread rain across Pakistan including Karachi
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has forecasted rainfall across several regions of Pakistan from March 24 to 30. Karachi and Hyderabad in Sindh are expected to receive showers on March 25,...

item.title: Defence argues Ontario couple tried to care for boy who later died in their home
item.description: Closing arguments are underway in Milton, Ontario, in the trial of Becky Hamber, 46, and Brandy Cooney, 44, a Burlington couple accused of first-degree murder and other charges in the death of a 12-ye...

item.title: Former CS Raphael Tuju Arrested After Voluntarily Reporting to Police
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju was arrested on Monday soon after he came out of a mysterious disappearance that had worried his family and legal team. Tuju had gone to a police station to give ...

item.title: Alex Eala eliminated by Karolina Muchova at Miami Open
item.description: Filipino tennis star Alex Eala was eliminated from the 2026 Miami Open after losing her match against Czech player Karolina Muchova. Despite showing flashes of strong performance, Eala struggled to ma...

item.title: Profile of Matt Brittin, Prospective BBC Director-General
item.description: Matt Brittin, a former executive at Google and accomplished sportsman, is widely expected to become the next director-general of the BBC, succeeding Tim Davie. Born in Walton-on-Thames, Surrey, Britti...

item.title: What Lies Beneath: Alice Faces Edward in Episode 111
item.description: Ang episode 111 ng ‘What Lies Beneath’ ay nagpakita kung paano nagkaroon ng tapang si Alice para harapin si Edward. Matapos ang mga pangyayari sa nakaraang mga episode, nagdesisyon siyang hindi na man...

item.title: PMD Forecasts Widespread Rain-Thunderstorms in Pakistan from March 24 to 30
item.description: Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has forecasted rainfall and thunderstorms across the country from March 24 to 30, with some gaps in between. A western disturbance will approach southwestern B...

item.title: Government urges researchers to address key social and economic challenges
item.description: DODOMA – Dr Tausi Kida, the Permanent Secretary of the President’s Office – Planning and Investment and Executive Secretary of the National Planning Commission, has called on all researchers in Tanzan...

item.title: E. P. Jayarajan comments on leaders leaving CPI(M) ahead of Kerala polls
item.description: Ahead of the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, senior CPI(M) leader E. P. Jayarajan addressed the media regarding recent departures of some party members. He stated that it is not unprecedented for lead...

item.title: Ride-hailing Strike, Trillanes Cyber Libel Case, Iran Tensions, and SB19’s Re-recording Highlight Today’s Top Stories
item.description: Rappler’s March 23, 2026 daily wrap features the day’s key developments both in the Philippines and internationally. Thousands of drivers from motorcycle and car ride-hailing apps such as Grab and Ang...

item.title: Trump Delays Military Strikes on Iran Amid Threat of Regional Retaliation
item.description: The US-Israel conflict with Iran, now in its fourth week, escalated fears of a wider regional crisis but saw a temporary de-escalation on March 23, 2026. US President Donald Trump announced a five-day...

item.title: Israeli Minister Proposes Extending Border to Litani River Amid Lebanon Strikes
item.description: On 23rd March 2026, Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich publicly called for extending Israel’s border to the Litani River in southern Lebanon, amid ongoing military operations. Israeli forces ha...

item.title: Matt Brittin to Become BBC Director General Amid High Expectations and Scrutiny
item.description: Matt Brittin, former President of Google in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, is widely expected to be appointed as the next Director General of the BBC. After nearly two decades at Google, Brittin ...

item.title: Air Canada plane collides with runway vehicle at LaGuardia Airport, killing two pilots and injuring dozens
item.description: Transcripts from the tragic crash between an Air Canada aircraft and a runway fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport have revealed that an air traffic controller admitted to having 'messed up' mom...

item.title: Media Control and the Illusion of Democratic Elections
item.description: This article, written in 2026, critiques the fairness of democratic elections, arguing that the system is inherently skewed by the control of media outlets by wealthy individuals and corporations. The...

item.title: Governor Dapo Abiodun’s Road Projects and Ogun State’s Infrastructure Transformation
item.description: Ogun State Governor Dapo Abiodun has made road infrastructure a central pillar of his administration since 2019, as part of a broader plan to transform the state’s economy and connectivity. The piece ...

item.title: APC Convention to Present Tinubu’s Performance and Environmental Agenda
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don release their plan for the National Convention wey dem wan hold this weekend. According to the party, the main aim na to review the work President Bola Tinubu d...

item.title: China’s Zero-Tariff Plan Opens New Trade Doors for African Economies
item.description: China’s decision to open its market to 53 African countries with zero tariffs beginning May 2026 marks a major shift in China-Africa trade relations. During his official visit to Kenya, China’s Vice P...
